identities.name: NCERT Direct Line Affirmation
identities.logic: Questions using exact sentences from NCERT where one word is swapped (e.g., 'primary' vs 'secondary').

identities.name: Matching Column Precision
identities.logic: Multi-category matching (Disease-Causative Agent-Vector). NEET often focuses on obscure protozoans.

identities.name: Assertion-Reasoning (A-R) Mechanism
identities.logic: Evaluating if 'R' explains 'A'. Trap: Both are true but R is just a related fact, not the explanation.

identities.name: Biotech Tools - Restriction Endonucleases
identities.logic: EcoRI recognition sequences and 'Sticky Ends' logic. Calculating fragment counts for linear vs circular DNA.

identities.name: Human Physiology - Regulatory Loops
identities.logic: Negative feedback loops of Hormones (ADH, RAAS mechanism). Focus on 'increase in blood pressure' vs 'decrease'.

identities.name: Botanical Taxonomy Exceptions
identities.logic: Exceptions in Gymnosperms and Pteridophytes (Heterspory). Focus on 'Salvinia and Selaginella'.

traps.name: Incorrect vs Correct Statement
traps.description: Asking 'How many of the above are INCORRECT?' forcing the student to re-read every statement carefully.

traps.name: Anatomy Label Flip
traps.description: Flipping labels of Ventral vs Dorsal surfaces in diagrams.
